summary: A publisher's blurb for Stuart Russell's book 'Human Compatible', outlining his core thesis that superhuman AI poses an existential threat to humanity. It highlights his proposed solution: reshaping the foundations of AI to ensure machines inherently pursue human objectives rather than their own.

principles.statement: The foundations of AI must be reshaped to guarantee that machines pursue human objectives, not their own.
principles.rationale: If we do not align AI with human objectives, we risk losing control of machines more powerful than we are, which poses an existential threat.
principles.source_quote: reshaping the foundations of AI to guarantee that machines pursue our objectives, not theirs

principles.statement: Creating superior intelligence could be the last event in human history if we fail to solve the control problem.
principles.rationale: While AI has an enormous capacity to improve our lives, creating machines more powerful than humans is an existential threat to our species if we lose control of them.
principles.source_quote: Creating superior intelligence would be the biggest event in human history. Unfortunately, according to the world's pre-eminent AI expert, it could also be the last.

anti_patterns.description: Building AI systems that pursue their own objectives rather than human objectives.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It leads to humanity losing control of machines that are more powerful than we are, resulting in an existential threat to the species.
